hero.available: Available for freelance — a few build slots open
hero.accent: AI agents, RAG pipelines, LLM copilots, AI-native apps
hero.subtitle: Software engineer by trade, freelance by night — retrieval, agents and automations wired into real products with the same discipline I bring to C#, .NET and Angular. Honest scope, calm delivery, code you own.

approach.label: The honest version
approach.body: By day I engineer enterprise software at KION. By night, on weekends and during holidays, I build freelance projects end to end — architecture, code and delivery from a single pair of hands. Every project on this site is something I designed and shipped myself, so you can judge the work, not the pitch.

capabilities.label: What I can build for you
capabilities.title: AI-native software, built by an engineer.
capabilities.subtitle: One engineer across the whole stack — so your product stays coherent from data model to interface, with AI wired in exactly where it earns its place.

AI.title: AI features that actually ship
AI.desc: Assistants, retrieval-augmented search, agents and automations — grounded in your data, typed, observable and integrated properly. Not a weekend demo: production.

ANGULAR.title: Web apps people enjoy using
ANGULAR.desc: Fast, accessible Angular front-ends with clean architecture — built so your team can keep extending them long after handover.

DOTNET.title: Backends built to last
DOTNET.desc: C# / .NET services, REST APIs and data models with Clean Architecture and CQRS — the kind of backend that survives audits and scale.

CPP.title: Performance when it's non-negotiable
CPP.desc: C++ modules, simulations and engine logic for when every millisecond and every byte counts. My roots, still sharp.

VR.title: Immersive VR & 3D
VR.desc: Real-time 3D, VR/AR prototypes and interactive visualisations — training sims, product configurators and spatial experiences built in Unity and WebGL.

agent.label: AI, in practice
agent.title: From prompt to shipped feature.
agent.prompt: Summarise this 40-page supplier contract and flag the risky clauses.

plan.title: Plan
plan.detail: Break the request into steps · pick the right tools

reason.title: Reason
reason.detail: Score every clause by risk · keep the citations

generate.title: Generate
generate.detail: Draft a plain-language summary and a redline

agent.result: 3 high-risk clauses found: auto-renewal (§7.2), unlimited liability (§11) and a 90-day termination lock-in (§14). Plain-language summary and a redline proposal are ready for review.
agent.caption: Typed end to end. Grounded in your documents. No hallucinated clauses.

ownership.title: Full-stack ownership
ownership.desc: Data model to API to interface to deployment — no handoffs, no blame gaps, no excuses.

ship.title: Ships on deadline
ship.desc: Real constraints, real clients, real production pressure. Familiar territory — I deliver.

direct.title: Direct line to the engineer
direct.desc: No account managers, no markup. You talk to the person actually writing the code.

ai.title: AI done honestly
ai.desc: Semantic Kernel, RAG, custom agents — and the honesty to tell you when you don't need them.

process.label: How we work together
process.title: From first message to shipped — no friction.
process.subtitle: A calm, predictable rhythm that keeps you in control the whole way through.

step1.title: Discovery
step1.desc: We scope the problem, define what success looks like, and agree a plan. You get a written proposal within 48 hours — no surprises later.

step2.title: Build in the open
step2.desc: Short, visible cycles with a running demo at every milestone. You see real progress every week, not status reports.

step3.title: Ship & support
step3.desc: Tested, documented and deployed. The codebase is yours with a clean handover — and I stick around to make sure it lands.

projects.subtitle: Selected freelance work and demos from the same stack I use in client projects.

faq.label: Good to know
faq.title: Questions clients usually ask.
faq.subtitle: If yours isn't here, just ask — I answer honestly and fast.

ai.a: Often, no — and I'll tell you when a simpler solution is cheaper and more maintainable. When AI genuinely fits (search, extraction, agents, automation) I build it grounded in your data, with guardrails and tests — not vibes.

pricing.a: Most projects are fixed-scope with a clear quote up front, or time-boxed at a day rate for open-ended work. You'll always know the price before I start — no surprise invoices.

timeline.q: How long does a project take?
timeline.a: A focused MVP is usually a few weeks; larger builds run in milestones so you get working software early and often. I give you a realistic timeline in the proposal, not an optimistic one.

comms.q: How do we stay in sync?
comms.a: Async-first: short written updates, decisions captured in writing, and a demo at every milestone. We hop on a call when it helps — but I won't fill your calendar with status meetings.

team.q: Can you work with my team or existing codebase?
team.a: Yes. I'm comfortable picking up an existing repo, following your conventions, and working alongside your developers — clear communication.

changes.a: They always do. Milestone-based delivery means we can re-prioritise between cycles without blowing the budget. You stay in control of what gets built next.

contact.cta_heading: Have an AI feature or a product in mind? Let's talk.
contact.note: I usually reply within one business day. No sales calls — just a short written exchange to see if we're a good fit.
